penalties.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out by the maximum time limit. The cutting appears to have stopped partway around the circle.

consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement between models on task completion. Claude reported incomplete circle at 300s timeout, while GPT reported completion at 115.2s with line deviation. Given the fundamental disagreement on whether the task was completed, and Claude's specific observation of cutting stopping partway, the incomplete task assessment is more defensible under the rubric's automatic-zero condition for non-completion.

disagreements.rationale: Task completion is binary - either the circle was cut out or it wasn't. Claude's observation of incomplete cutting at timeout is more consistent with the automatic-zero penalty structure.
Incomplete circle penalty takes precedence over line deviation penalties according to rubric hierarchy. If the circle wasn't completed, line deviation becomes moot.
If the circle was not completed as determined above, then circle_release phase could not have occurred.
Incomplete circle penalty results in automatic zero score according to rubric for precision cutting task.
